questions.prompt: What should the interactive configure experience feel like?
questions.description: The current flow asks for new entries linearly and then rewrites selected files. The goal implies a real editor-like session.
questions.recommendedAnswer: A manifest editor session: choose a manifest type, see existing entries, then choose add, edit, remove, duplicate, or finish, with a final preview before writing.

questions.prompt: Which manifest types should be editable in this goal?
questions.description: The repo currently has rules, skills, MCPs, utils, hooks, and presets. Presets are shown but not part of configure today.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Support full editing for rules, skills, MCPs, utils, and hooks. Include presets if the same shared editor model makes it low-risk; otherwise leave presets as a follow-up.

questions.prompt: What operations must the first version support?
questions.description: This decides whether the feature is only append/remove or a more complete editor.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Add, edit, remove, toggle default fields, toggle autoInvocation where applicable, preview all changes, and confirm before writing.

questions.prompt: How open should we be to changing the prompt/UI stack?
questions.description: The project already uses @inquirer/prompts and custom themes. A richer TUI could help, but adds dependency and maintenance cost.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Start with the existing @inquirer/prompts stack and design a better menu loop first. Only add a new TUI library if we find a specific UX requirement that inquirer cannot satisfy cleanly.

questions.prompt: What safety and validation should the editor guarantee?
questions.description: This feature writes setup manifests that later execute commands, fetch URLs, and merge hooks.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Never write without preview and confirmation; validate manifest shape before writing; protect against duplicate ids; make removals explicit; preserve existing valid fields when editing; support --dry-run.

questions.prompt: What should happen to the current configure behavior and flags?
questions.description: Today --from-current changes append behavior and --local chooses project manifests.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Keep command names and flags compatible, but make the default configure flow load existing manifests when present. Treat creating from scratch as an explicit option inside the session or behind a new flag.
